Metadata.title: SBOMHub - CRA-ready AI Compliance Evidence Layer
Metadata.description: Dependency-Track finds CVEs. SBOMHub turns them into submittable VEX, CRA reports, and audit trails. For Japanese SMB manufacturers facing the EU Cyber Resilience Act 2026/9 deadline. AGPL-3.0 OSS with BYOK LLM.
Metadata.keywords: SBOM, CRA, EU Cyber Resilience Act, vulnerability reporting, VEX, CycloneDX, Dependency-Track, Syft, Trivy, METI, BYOK, self-hosted, AGPL, AI, SMB manufacturers, IoT, embedded
Metadata.ogTitle: SBOMHub - Turn SBOMs into submittable CRA reports, VEX, and audit trails
Metadata.ogDescription: For Japanese SMB manufacturers facing CRA 2026/9. AI drafts VEX, CRA reports, and METI self-assessments from your existing SBOM tools. Humans approve. BYOK LLM.
Metadata.twitterTitle: SBOMHub - CRA-ready AI Compliance Evidence Layer
Metadata.twitterDescription: Dependency-Track finds CVEs. SBOMHub turns them into submittable artefacts. AI VEX/report drafts for CRA 2026/9.

Landing.sunsetBanner: The hosted SaaS (sbomhub.app) is sunset while we transition to the new positioning. New plan signups are paused. See

hero.badge: CRA 2026/9 ready - AI Compliance Evidence Layer
hero.title:
Dependency-Track finds CVEs.
SBOMHub turns them into submittable artefacts.
hero.subtitle: Convert SBOMs into submittable VEX, CRA reports, and audit trails. Built for Japanese SMB manufacturers shipping IoT / embedded products to the EU.

target.title: For Japanese SMB manufacturers shipping IoT / embedded products to the EU
target.description: If your team has no dedicated PSIRT and handles SBOM, VEX, and CRA reporting as a side job, SBOMHub ingests Syft / Trivy / Dependency-Track output, drafts the human work with AI, and lets you approve submittable artefacts.

problem.title: What CRA 2026/9 actually breaks
problem.description: The EU CRA starts enforcing vulnerability reporting on 11 September 2026: 24 hours for early warning after exploitation is known, 72 hours for detailed notification. Non-compliance: up to EUR 15M or 2.5% of global annual turnover.

items.title: Too many CVEs from your scanners
items.description: Syft / Trivy / DT surface CVEs but never decide impact. Dozens of Critical and High per month per product is unsustainable for a non-PSIRT team.

items.title: VEX is unfamiliar
items.description: CycloneDX VEX's analysis.state and justification fields require careful reasoning. Mis-labelling 'not_affected' is a legal liability under CRA, not just a security mistake.

items.title: No one to draft CRA reports
items.description: 24h early warning, 72h detailed notification, final report. Required in JP and EN. Doing this without a dedicated PSIRT and without templates is unrealistic.

items.title: Code can't leave your network
items.description: Manufacturers cannot send proprietary source or detailed SBOMs to overseas SaaS LLMs. You need BYOK or fully local LLM, and a Japanese-language UI for compliance teams.

items.title: METI self-assessment is all manual
items.description: Japan's METI SBOM guideline self-assessment is a hand-written checklist today. Prefilling it from CI configs and scan history would cut the work by an order of magnitude.

solution.title: AI Compliance Evidence Pack
solution.description: Ingest output from Syft / Trivy / Dependency-Track. AI drafts VEX, CRA reports, and METI self-assessments. Humans approve every output. Every draft and decision is recorded in the audit log.
solution.principle: AI drafts. Humans decide. Every output carries confidence and evidence; below the threshold, the draft falls back to under_investigation.

evidencePack.title: VEX drafts
evidencePack.description: AI proposes affected / not_affected / under_investigation / resolved with code reachability and advisory excerpts as evidence.

evidencePack.title: CRA early warning (24h)
evidencePack.description: Template draft for the 24-hour early warning, prefilled with product, CVE, exploitation status, and provisional impact scope.

evidencePack.title: CRA detailed notification (72h)
evidencePack.description: Affected components, target versions, mitigations, and remediation plan drafted by AI for the 72-hour submission.

evidencePack.title: CRA final report
evidencePack.description: Final report draft covering permanent fix, recurrence prevention, and patched versions. JP and EN drafts side by side.

evidencePack.title: METI self-assessment prefill
evidencePack.description: Pre-fills METI's SBOM management self-assessment from CI configs, SBOM scan history, and correlation history.

evidencePack.title: SBOM / VEX export
evidencePack.description: Submittable CycloneDX, SPDX, and VEX files with evidence attached for traceability and audit.

evidencePack.title: Shareable links
evidencePack.description: Generate a controlled link to share the Evidence Pack with customers and auditors, with expiry and scope controls.

evidencePack.title: Audit trail
evidencePack.description: AI draft, human edits, approval / rejection, and final submission are all recorded. Use it for CRA inspection and METI explanations.

byok.title: AI = BYOK (Bring Your Own Key)
byok.description: SBOMHub's open-source build ships zero LLM credentials. Choose your own LLM provider: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Azure OpenAI, or Ollama for a fully local model. M4 will harden Local LLM quality for manufacturers who cannot send code outside.
byok.selfHostNote: Self-hosted deployments can lock the configuration to BYOK only or Local LLM only.

selfHost.title: Self-host in five minutes
selfHost.description: Docker Compose, AGPL-3.0, all features. Built for manufacturers who keep code inside the company network.
selfHost.command:
git clone https://github.com/youichi-uda/sbomhub.git
cd sbomhub
docker compose up -d
# -> http://localhost:3000
selfHost.dockerHint: For production, run the Trust Rescue steps first: initialise an encryption key and split the migration / runtime Postgres roles.

cli.title: CLI for local analysis
cli.description: sbomhub-cli wraps Syft / Trivy / cdxgen with auto-detection. Scan, AI triage, and VEX export in one command.
cli.command:
sbomhub scan . --project my-device
sbomhub triage . --project my-device --ecosystem go
sbomhub vex export --project my-device --output vex.json
sbomhub cra draft --project my-device --cve CVE-XXXX-YYYY --lang ja,en

roadmap.title: Roadmap
roadmap.description: Worked back from CRA 2026-09-11. The Trust Rescue work (RLS, encryption keys, API contracts, CI, distribution) runs ahead of M0/M1.

milestones.title: Strategy lock-in + Trust Rescue
milestones.description: New positioning across README/LP. CRA SMB landing + waitlist. Begin RLS, key, API contract, distribution, and CI P0 fixes.

milestones.title: AI VEX triage MVP
milestones.description: Go / npm reachability, LLM judgement, VEX draft persistence, approve / edit / reject UI, CycloneDX VEX export, confidence + evidence + audit log.

milestones.title: CRA report drafting
milestones.description: 24h early warning / 72h detailed notification / final report templates plus AI drafts. JP and EN drafts integrated into the Evidence Pack.

milestones.title: METI self-assessment prefill
milestones.description: Classify self-assessment items as met / unmet / needs review based on CI configs and SBOMHub usage, with evidence and remediation.

milestones.title: Local LLM / Enterprise self-host polish
milestones.description: BYO key abstraction, Ollama and local-LLM rollout, managed vs local quality benchmark, Enterprise commercial license and SLA collateral.

waitlist.title: Join the waitlist
waitlist.description: We will email you when AI VEX MVP, CRA report drafting, and METI self-assessment prefill ship. Tell us briefly what CRA work is hurting you and we will prioritise contact.
waitlist.success: Thanks. We will reach out from hello@sbomhub.app as features ship.
waitlist.error: Submission failed. Please try again later or email hello@sbomhub.app directly.
waitlist.fallbackNote: Your mail client will open. Send the prefilled message to finish signing up.

faqCta.title: Want the full strategy?
faqCta.description: The detailed reboot plan, market timing, and Evidence Pack specs live in the GitHub repository.

footer.description: AI compliance evidence layer for Japanese SMB manufacturers facing CRA 2026/9. AGPL-3.0.

Sunset.title: The SBOMHub SaaS (sbomhub.app) is currently sunset
Sunset.subtitle: We are repositioning SBOMHub as a CRA 2026/9-ready AI compliance evidence layer. While the transition is in progress, we are not accepting new signups or new plan checkouts on the hosted SaaS.
Sunset.selfHostDescription: SBOMHub OSS (AGPL-3.0) is on GitHub. Docker Compose brings it up in five minutes and provides the same feature set the SaaS did. For AI features, configure a BYOK LLM provider or a local Ollama-compatible endpoint.
Sunset.waitlistTitle: Get notified when new modules ship
Sunset.waitlistDescription: Join the waitlist to be notified when AI VEX MVP, CRA report drafting, and METI self-assessment prefill are released.
Sunset.existingTitle: Existing subscription, cancellation, and data export
Sunset.existingDescription: If you have an active subscription, want to cancel it, or need a data export or migration, email hello@sbomhub.app. We respond within one business day.

SSVC.title: SSVC Assessment

decisionDescription.immediate: Stop all other work and address this vulnerability immediately
decisionDescription.out_of_cycle: Address this vulnerability outside of your regular update cycle as soon as possible
decisionDescription.scheduled: Plan to address this vulnerability during your next regular maintenance cycle
decisionDescription.defer: No action required at this time. Continue monitoring the situation

exploitationDescription.none: No exploit code or PoC has been identified
exploitationDescription.poc: Proof of concept exploit code is publicly available
exploitationDescription.active: Active exploitation has been observed in the wild

automatableDescription.no: Manual intervention is required for exploitation

technicalImpactDescription.partial: Some functionality or data is affected
technicalImpactDescription.total: Complete control of the system may be compromised

missionPrevalenceDescription.minimal: Affected systems have little relevance to the mission
missionPrevalenceDescription.essential: Affected systems are essential to the mission

safetyImpactDescription.significant: May significantly impact human life or safety

MCPDocs.title: MCP Server Integration Guide
MCPDocs.description: Access SBOMHub vulnerability information using natural language from Claude Desktop or Cursor.
MCPDocs.whatIsMcpDescription: MCP is a standard protocol that allows AI tools like Claude Desktop and Cursor to integrate with external services. Once you configure the SBOMHub MCP Server, you can check your project's vulnerability information just by talking to Claude.
MCPDocs.whatIsMcpExample: Example: Just say "Search for projects affected by CVE-2021-44228" to instantly check the impact of Log4Shell.
MCPDocs.step1Item4: Click "Create API Key" and copy the displayed key (sbh_...)
MCPDocs.step1Item2Revised: Go to Settings > API Keys in the sidebar
MCPDocs.step1Item3Revised: Click "Create API Key" and copy the displayed key (sbh_...)
MCPDocs.step4Description: Restart Claude Desktop / Cursor to enable SBOMHub tools.
MCPDocs.toolSearchCve: Cross-search all projects by CVE ID
MCPDocs.toolDiff: Compare two SBOMs and get the diff
MCPDocs.toolGetVulnerabilities: Get vulnerability list for a project
MCPDocs.usageExamplesDescription: Just talk to Claude to get information
MCPDocs.example3: "Show me the Critical vulnerabilities in my-app project"
MCPDocs.example5: "Check the compliance score for my project"
MCPDocs.error1Solution: The environment variable SBOMHUB_API_KEY is not set. Check your configuration file.
MCPDocs.error2Solution: Make sure pnpm build succeeded, dist/index.js exists, the path in config is correct, then restart the app.
MCPDocs.error3Solution: Check if the API key is correct, not expired, and the SBOMHub URL is correct.

Integrations.step1Description: Set up a connection to Jira, Backlog, or GitHub Issues using the form above. You'll need an API token (for GitHub, a personal access token with Issues read/write on the target repository).
Integrations.step2Description: Click the "Create Ticket" button from the vulnerability list or detail page to create an issue.
Integrations.step3Description: Ticket status is automatically synced. When an issue is resolved, the vulnerability status is also updated.

LLM.providerSectionDescription: Select a provider and supply the corresponding API key. Switching providers does not delete previously configured keys.
LLM.apiKeyPlaceholderConfigured: Leave unchanged to keep the existing key, or paste a new key to rotate
LLM.apiKeyHelp: Stored encrypted on the server. Once saved it is replaced by *** in API responses and cannot be retrieved.
LLM.apiKeyRequired: An API key is required for this provider.
LLM.modelHelp: Provider-specific model identifier. Leave blank to use the provider's default.
LLM.statusUnconfigured: No API key configured — AI features are disabled.

APIKeys.usageDescription: Use your API key with the following integrations

DependencyPath.title: Dependency path
DependencyPath.description: How this component is pulled into your project — every dependency chain from the root to this component. For a transitive dependency, the direct dependency at the start of each chain is what you upgrade to remove or update it.
DependencyPath.directHint: Upgrade or replace it directly in your project's manifest.
DependencyPath.transitiveHint: It is pulled in through the chains below. To change it, upgrade the direct dependency (in blue) at the start of each chain.
DependencyPath.truncated: Showing the first {count} paths. More paths exist but were truncated to keep this view responsive.
DependencyPath.truncatedEmpty: Dependency paths were too complex to enumerate within the computation budget. This component is present in the dependency graph, but its full set of entry paths could not be computed.
DependencyPath.degraded: Dependency edges are not available for this SBOM (e.g. SPDX). Path-to-root analysis requires a CycloneDX SBOM with a dependencies graph.
DependencyPath.empty: This component was not found in the dependency graph for the selected SBOM.

TransitiveImpact.title: Transitive entry paths
TransitiveImpact.subtitle: For each affected project, how the vulnerable component enters the dependency graph (root → … → component).
TransitiveImpact.directHint: This is a direct dependency — upgrade it directly in this project's manifest.
TransitiveImpact.transitiveHint: This is a transitive dependency — upgrade the direct dependency (in blue) at the start of each chain to change it.
TransitiveImpact.degraded: Dependency edges are unavailable for this project's SBOM format (e.g. SPDX). The affected components are listed, but their entry paths cannot be computed without a CycloneDX dependencies graph.
TransitiveImpact.notInGraph: This component is not present in the project's latest SBOM (it appeared only in an earlier snapshot), so it has no current entry path.
TransitiveImpact.truncated: Showing {count} paths. More paths exist but were truncated to keep this view responsive.
TransitiveImpact.truncatedEmpty: This component is reachable, but its entry paths were too complex to enumerate within the computation budget.
TransitiveImpact.noPaths: No entry path could be computed for this component.

Page.title: SBOM Change History
Page.description: Track supply-chain churn between SBOM ingests for this project. AI is intentionally not involved here — the diff is a mechanical comparison so the audit trail is deterministic.
Page.loadFailed: Failed to load the SBOM history
Page.noSboms: This project has no SBOMs yet. Upload an SBOM to start tracking changes.
Page.singleSbomBanner: Only one SBOM has been ingested. The detail view below treats every component as added (initial baseline). Upload another SBOM to compare revisions.

Timeline.title: SBOM revisions
Timeline.description: Newest first. Each row links to the diff against the previous revision.

AiSummary.title: AI Summary
AiSummary.description: AI extracts CRA-relevant findings, licence violations, and high-impact changes from the diff. Drafts only — humans approve.
AiSummary.idleHint: Click Generate to ask the LLM for a 3-5 sentence summary. When BYOK is not configured, a deterministic placeholder is shown instead.
AiSummary.disabledBanner: AI summary is disabled. Configure BYOK at /settings/llm to enable.

Graph.title: Dependency graph
Graph.description: Visualises the dependency relationships between the two SBOMs with diff colours (green: added / red: removed / amber: version changed / grey: unchanged). Parent → child edges are parsed from the CycloneDX dependencies field.
Graph.loadFailed: Failed to load the dependency graph
Graph.empty: No dependency relationships to render. Make sure a CycloneDX SBOM has been uploaded.

AIDisabledBanner.title: AI features are disabled
AIDisabledBanner.description: Configure an LLM provider in Settings > LLM to enable AI-assisted VEX triage, CRA report drafting, and METI self-assessment prefill.

Page.title: AI VEX triage
Page.description: AI-drafted VEX statements awaiting human approval. Every decision is recorded in the audit log.
Page.emptyDescription: There are no AI drafts waiting for a decision. Run a new triage cycle from the CLI ('sbomhub triage') or via the API to populate this queue.
Page.hiddenEvidenceless: {count} draft(s) hidden — no evidence attached. This should not happen; check the API logs.

DraftCard.lowConfidence: Below the {threshold}% threshold — surfaced as under_investigation.
DraftCard.notePlaceholder: Why are you editing this draft?
DraftCard.rejectReasonPlaceholder: Optional — why is this AI draft wrong?
DraftCard.addToCRADisabled: CRA report drafting lands in M2

CrossProject.title: Decided in other projects
CrossProject.description: Human-approved VEX statements for the same finding from other projects in your organisation. Review the provenance, then reuse a decision with one click — you confirm each reuse, and it is recorded in the audit log.
CrossProject.matchPurlHint: The source statement targets the same component (matched by purl) — a precise match.
CrossProject.matchVulnerabilityOnlyHint: The source statement is component-agnostic; matched on the CVE alone — a coarser match, review carefully.
CrossProject.applyConfirmBody: Copy {project}'s decision ({status}) into this project? This records a VEX statement here, with provenance back to the source. You can change it afterwards like any other statement.
CrossProject.applyError: Could not reuse this decision. Please try again.
CrossProject.reuseNote: Reuse copies the source decision into this project only after you confirm; every reuse is recorded in the audit log.

Page.title: AI CRA report drafts
Page.description: AI-drafted CRA reports (24h early warning / 72h detailed notification / final report) awaiting human approval. Every decision is recorded in the audit log. Submission to the regulator lands in M3; today this surface manages the drafts only.
Page.emptyDescription: There are no AI drafts waiting for a decision. Run a new CRA drafting cycle from the CLI ('sbomhub cra draft') or via the API to populate this queue. At least one approved VEX draft for the target CVE is required first.
Page.hiddenEvidenceless: {count} report(s) hidden — no evidence attached. This should not happen; check the API logs.
Page.truncated: Showing {visible} of {total} reports. Narrow the filters or process the top of the queue first.
Page.setAwarenessFailed: Failed to update the awareness time

ReportCard.notePlaceholder: Why are you editing this draft?
ReportCard.submitDisabled: Only approved reports can be recorded as submitted
ReportCard.authorityPlaceholder: e.g. national CSIRT / ENISA single reporting platform
ReportCard.referenceNumberPlaceholder: Acknowledgement or case number from the authority
ReportCard.submissionNotesPlaceholder: Anything worth recording about this submission
ReportCard.rejectReasonPlaceholder: Optional — why is this AI draft wrong?
ReportCard.awarenessHint: The Art.14 clock start. Must not be in the future; clearing it unsets the deadline (degrades to not applicable).
ReportCard.alreadyDecided: This report has already been marked as {decision}. A new decision will overwrite the audit trail.

Page.title: METI SBOM self-assessment
Page.description: Auto-scores the METI 'Guide for Introducing SBOM for Software Management ver 2.0' self-assessment checklist ({total} items) from the project's SBOM / vulnerability / VEX / CRA history. Every row can be manually overridden, and all actions are recorded in the audit log.
Page.emptyDescription: Press 'Re-evaluate' to score all 32 criteria from the project's SBOM / vulnerability / VEX / CRA history. Each row can then be manually overridden.

CriterionCard.evidenceEmpty: No evidence (normal for needs_review / not_applicable rows)
CriterionCard.overrideFormNotePlaceholder: Why are you overriding the evaluator verdict? (e.g. vendor attestation reviewed)
CriterionCard.overrideFormImprovementPlaceholder: Next step for this criterion (leave blank to keep existing)
CriterionCard.overrideFormImprovementHint: Leaving this empty keeps the existing improvement action. To clear it, enter a single space, or use 'Clear override' to drop the override entirely.
CriterionCard.overrideValidationNote: Note must be 2000 characters or fewer
CriterionCard.clearOverrideConfirm: This drops the manual override on this row and reverts to the evaluator verdict. The rationale (why the override is no longer needed) is recorded in the audit log.
CriterionCard.clearOverrideNotePlaceholder: Why are you clearing the override? (e.g. re-evaluated, the original override was wrong)
CriterionCard.clearOverrideValidationNote: Clear rationale must be 1..4096 characters (whitespace-only is not accepted)

FAQ.title: Frequently Asked Questions
FAQ.a1: The EU Cyber Resilience Act (CRA) is a regulation that imposes vulnerability reporting obligations on manufacturers of digital products (including IoT and embedded) sold in the EU. It entered into force on 2024-12-10, and the vulnerability and incident reporting obligations apply from 2026-09-11: an early warning within 24 hours of an actively exploited vulnerability becoming known, a detailed notification within 72 hours, and a final report. Penalties for non-compliance can reach EUR 15M or 2.5% of global annual turnover.
FAQ.a2: VEX (Vulnerability Exploitability eXchange) is a standard format for declaring whether a reported vulnerability actually affects your product. CycloneDX VEX uses analysis.state values (not_affected / affected / under_investigation / resolved), a justification (e.g., code_not_present, code_not_reachable), and a detail explanation. In SBOMHub, AI drafts each VEX statement and a human approves, edits, or rejects it - a strict human-in-the-loop workflow.
FAQ.q3: What does BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) mean here?
FAQ.a3: SBOMHub OSS / self-hosted does not ship any LLM API keys. The operator configures one of OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Azure OpenAI, or Ollama (local). Manufacturers who cannot send code to overseas APIs can pick Ollama and keep all AI work inside the company network. AI features use the configured provider only; no traffic is sent to SBOMHub.
FAQ.q4: How does self-host differ from the SaaS?
FAQ.a4: The SBOMHub SaaS at sbomhub.app is currently sunset while we transition to the new positioning and is not accepting new signups. The OSS self-host build (AGPL-3.0) is fully supported, can be started with Docker Compose in about five minutes, and has the same feature set the SaaS had. The new SaaS reopening will be announced via the GitHub repository and the waitlist.
FAQ.a5: Japan's METI publishes 'Guide for Introducing SBOM for Software Management', which contains a self-assessment checklist for an organisation's SBOM management practices. In M3, SBOMHub will auto-prefill these items from your CI configuration, SBOM generation history, and vulnerability correlation history, classifying each as met / unmet / needs review with evidence and remediation suggestions. This is not an official compliance certification, but a tool that supports the self-assessment work.

SettingsDiffWebhook.title: Diff Webhook Settings
SettingsDiffWebhook.description: Notify a destination URL with an HMAC-SHA256 signature when an SBOM ingest produces a diff exceeding configured severity thresholds.
SettingsDiffWebhook.securityDescription: The webhook secret is AES-256-GCM encrypted at rest. Plaintext is never re-displayed.
SettingsDiffWebhook.destinationDescription: Configure URL, secret, format, and thresholds.
SettingsDiffWebhook.urlHelp: A Slack incoming-webhook URL or any HTTP endpoint that accepts POST.
SettingsDiffWebhook.urlRequired: URL is required when the webhook is enabled.
SettingsDiffWebhook.urlProtocol: URL must start with http:// or https://.
SettingsDiffWebhook.secretHelp: Receivers should verify the X-SBOMHub-Signature header (sha256=<hex>) using HMAC-SHA256(secret, body).
SettingsDiffWebhook.formatHelp: The Slack format embeds the canonical SBOMHub payload alongside Slack-shaped text + attachments.
SettingsDiffWebhook.thresholdsHelp: Fires when the new-item count reaches the threshold. 0 = trigger on any item (licenses only).
SettingsDiffWebhook.autoTriggerNote: Auto-trigger on SBOM ingest is not yet implemented (M12 follow-up). The test-fire button verifies the configuration.
